name: TAM Layering: Irrealis Mood
intuition: The situation is construed as projected, hypothetical, imagined, desired, or otherwise non-actual. It is not asserted as a realized fact in the actual world.
set_to_1_if: The gram contains a modal auxiliary (e.g., 'will', 'would', 'could', 'should', 'may', 'might') that presents the situation as projected, hypothetical, or uncertain rather than realized.
The gram appears in a conditional or counterfactual construction (e.g., 'If he had known...', '...he would have gone').
The gram occurs in a clause expressing desire, obligation, or hypothetical evaluation (e.g., optative or subjunctive contexts).
The gram is marked as future (e.g., 'He will have finished'), presenting the situation as not yet realized.
set_to_0_if: The gram asserts the situation as realized in the actual world, and none of the modal, conditional, future, or wish contexts apply.
edge_cases: Negation alone does not trigger irrealis. 'He has not eaten' asserts a realized state of affairs in the actual world. By contrast, 'I hope he has not eaten' embeds the clause within a domain of desire rather than assertion.

Interrogative form alone does not trigger irrealis. A question seeking confirmation of an actual fact (e.g., 'Has he arrived?') is realis. However, a question that projects a future or hypothetical outcome (e.g., 'What will he have done by then?') is irrealis.
examples_positive: He will have finished by noon. (Future/prediction -> 1)
examples_negative: He has finished. (Asserted as realized in the actual world -> 0)
